title.purpose: Page-level headers, e.g. the Dashboard greeting.

subtitle.purpose: Form labels, card titles, the logged-in user's name.

label.purpose: Helper text, field validation messages, role tags.

shadows.name: card-lift
shadows.purpose: Always-on ambient lift for cards and the login panel. Not a hover effect.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Default easing for color and background transitions on hover/focus (150ms).

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Main call-to-action button, e.g. login submit.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #2563eb; color: #ffffff; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 600; padding: 8px 16px; border: none; border-radius: 6px; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #1d4ed8; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #2563eb; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.6; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Bordered ghost button for lower-emphasis actions, e.g. logout.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { background: #ffffff; color: #475569; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; padding: 6px 12px; border: 1px solid #cbd5e1; border-radius: 6px; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: #f8fafc; } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #2563eb; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Input Field
components.description: Labeled text input with a single-ring focus treatment.
components.css: .ds-field { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 4px; } .ds-field-label { font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; color: #334155; } .ds-field-input { background: #ffffff; color: #1e293b; font-size: 0.875rem; padding: 8px 12px; border: 1px solid #cbd5e1; border-radius: 6px; } .ds-field-input:focus-visible { outline: none; border-color: #3b82f6; box-shadow: 0 0 0 1px #3b82f6; }

components.name: Sidebar Nav Item
components.description: Dark sidebar navigation link, default and active states.
components.html: DashboardMatérias-primas
components.css: .ds-nav { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 4px; background: #0f172a; padding: 12px; } .ds-nav-item { display: block; padding: 8px 12px; border-radius: 6px; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; color: #cbd5e1; text-decoration: none; transition: background-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1), color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-nav-item:hover { background: #1e293b; color: #f1f5f9; } .ds-nav-item-active { background: #2563eb; color: #ffffff; }

components.name: Stat Card
components.description: Dashboard indicator tile: label plus a value that shows an em-dash until real data exists.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #ffffff; border: 1px solid #e2e8f0; border-radius: 8px; padding: 20px; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px 0 rgb(0 0 0 / 0.05); } .ds-card-label { font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; color: #64748b; margin: 0; } .ds-card-value { font-size: 1.5rem; font-weight: 600; color: #1e293b; margin: 8px 0 0; }

components.name: Empty State Card
components.description: Dashed-border placeholder for sections not built yet (Produção, Pedidos, Romaneios, etc.) — never fabricated data.
components.html:
Produção

Esta seção será implementada na próxima etapa.
components.css: .ds-empty { background: #ffffff; border: 1px dashed #cbd5e1; border-radius: 8px; padding: 40px; text-align: center; } .ds-empty-title { font-size: 1.125rem; font-weight: 600; color: #1e293b; margin: 0; } .ds-empty-body { font-size: 0.875rem; color: #64748b; margin: 8px 0 0; }

narrative.overview:
Meu Inventário reads like a well-kept ledger, not a SaaS dashboard: flat surfaces, one accent color reserved strictly for action and status, and typography that carries all the hierarchy on its own. The system exists to get a solo workshop owner in, informed, and back to work — every visual decision is judged against that five-second stock check, not against how a screenshot would look in a portfolio.

It deliberately rejects the inventory-SaaS playbook: no decorative hero-metric tiles standing in for real data, no dense enterprise data-grids built for teams of analysts, no ceremony (extra clicks, confirmation modals, decorative motion) wrapped around simple daily actions.
keyCharacteristics: Flat by default — a single subtle shadow, used only on cards, is the entire elevation vocabulary
One accent (blue) reserved for actionable and active elements; never decorative
A single system-UI font stack, hierarchy carried by weight/size/color alone
A dark, utilitarian sidebar as the one deliberate contrast surface
Generous but efficient whitespace — no filler, no empty ornamentation

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: Blue appears only on things you can act on or that are currently active: a submit button, the active nav item, a focus ring. If it's not clickable or current, it isn't blue.

rules.name: The Single Family Rule
rules.body: One font family end to end. A second typeface would read as decoration this system explicitly rejects — hierarchy is a weight/size/color problem, never a font-pairing problem.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces do not gain shadow on hover or focus. If a state needs to read as active, change background or border color — never add elevation to communicate it.

dos: Do keep every screen flat: Card Lift is the only shadow allowed, applied at rest, never intensified on hover.
Do reserve Ledger Blue for actionable or active elements only (buttons, active nav, focus rings).
Do carry hierarchy with weight/size/color inside the single system-UI font family.
Do use the dashed-border empty-state pattern for unbuilt sections instead of fabricating placeholder data.
donts: Don't add decorative hero-metric cards with numbers that aren't backed by real data — Meu Inventário's stat tiles show an em-dash until there's a real value, never a fake number for polish.
Don't build dense, analyst-style data-grids. This is a solo-owner tool, not a BI console.
Don't add ceremony — confirmation modals, extra clicks, or decorative motion — around simple daily actions like checking or logging stock.
Don't introduce a second typeface or a display/hero type size; there is no marketing surface here to justify one.
Don't use border-left/border-right as a colored accent stripe anywhere in the system.
